stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Sgraffito is a ceramic technique in which a layer of slip is applied over a contrasting clay body and selectively scratched away to reveal an image.
  • Magdalene Odundo is a Kenyan-born British ceramicist who uses sgraffito in some of her work.
  • “By incising lines through the surface, a sense of the breath of the vessel emerges,” she says about sgraffito.
  • Her work often draws on female figural forms, which she describes as “quiet witnesses of generational memory.”
  • Her sgraffito vessel “Untitled, Asymmetric Series II” (1994) features incised vertical lines that ripple along a leaning ovoid form.
stem: The student wants to provide a quotation from Odundo that explains why she used sgraffito in “Untitled, Asymmetric Series II.” Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Sgraffito, the technique used in Odundo's “Untitled, Asymmetric Series II,” involves applying slip over a contrasting clay body and scratching away portions to reveal an image.
“Untitled, Asymmetric Series II” presents the female figural form as Odundo sees it: a “quiet witness of generational memory.”

In making “Untitled, Asymmetric Series II,” Odundo applied sgraffito to create what she called a “sense of the breath of the vessel” through the incised vertical lines rippling along the leaning ovoid form.
In “Untitled, Asymmetric Series II,” Odundo uses a technique she says emerges by “incising lines through the surface.”

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence provides Odundo's quotation about the “sense of the breath of the vessel” to explain that she used sgraffito to create that effect through the incised vertical lines on her vessel.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence explains sgraffito and indicates that Odundo used the technique, but it does not provide a quotation or explain why. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence provides a quotation from Odundo, the quotation illustrates her views on the female figural form; the sentence does not explain why she used sgraffito. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence provides a quotation about the technique, the quotation merely describes a specific aspect of sgraffito; the sentence does not explain why Odundo used sgraffito in the vessel.
